items.title: US military strikes Iranian boats and missile launch sites

children.content: US Centcom says the strikes were designed "to protect our troops from threats posed by Iranian forces".

articles.title: Australian stocks fall as US strikes dampen investor sentiment

children.content: The local share market is in the red, while shares in stock exchange operator ASX Ltd have tumbled on a trading update. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

articles.title: Trump calls on Arab nations to 'immediately sign the Abraham Accords'

children.content: Donald Trump has boldly tied negotiations to end his war with Iran to a swathe of Arab nations formally establishing diplomatic ties with Israel.

image.altText: Israeli PM Netanyahu shakes hands with US President Donald Trump, who points at him.

articles.title: Inside the Tehran press conference where the ABC was the only Western media present

children.content: While on assignment inside Iran, our reporter asked a foreign ministry official about the regime's plan for the Strait of Hormuz. The spokesperson responded that "there is no toll" but also signalled crossing the strait will not be entirely free.

image.altText: ABC reporter Karishma Vyas sits among other reporters and asks a question

items.title: SA Police return to Yunta as part of Gus Lamont investigation

image.altText: Gus Lamont

items.title: Power price drops for most customers as batteries flatten peak

children.content: Surging levels of renewable energy and better reliability from coal-fired generators are set to give consumers a break, with benchmark power prices to fall up to 10 per cent for consumers and more for small businesses.

image.altText: rows of battery containers at sunset

items.title: Leaked documents reveal how far BHP really is from net zero goals

children.content: The mining giant's delay in the rollout of renewable energy and a fleet of electric trucks and trains in the Pilbara has raised doubts inside the company about it meeting its ambitious climate goal.

image.altText: A large yellow mining truck drives along a raise road. It has a load in its tray.

articles.title: Israeli politician calls for investigation after Gaza flotilla activists allege abuse

image.altText: Yair Lapid speaks at a lectern gesturing with one hand.

articles.title: Labor to ignore call for pause on CGT changes

articles.title: Government sets up fight with Santos over gas reservation plan

children.content: The draft framework outlines a requirement for gas producers to supply 20 per cent of their export to the domestic market, which the gas lobby argues will "undermine" the industry.

items.title: Man's death confirmed as diphtheria-related after overseas toxicology test

children.content: The Northern Territory government says the rate of new diphtheria cases in the territory has fallen from more than 20 per week to less than 10 after 10,000 Territorians stepped up to be immunised against the ongoing outbreak.

items.title: How to check if your vaccines are up to date as diphtheria outbreak continues

items.title: 'Waiting for them to die': Former commissioner slams government inaction
